advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Add teardown that removes the throttle marker for the test tracker (e.g. an autouse cleanup or expire in a fixture finalizer).
evidence: `_expire_marker` only removes before each test: `def _expire_marker(tracker: Path) -> None:\n    with contextlib.suppress(FileNotFoundError):\n        os.remove(_marker_path(tracker))`
No fixture teardown / `finally` removes the marker written by `ensure_fresh` (reads.py:234)
Marker path is process-global `/tmp/.ticket-sync-<md5>`, keyed on the per-test tmp_path realpath
Registrar binds `_readonly = ctx.readonly` (_mcp_reads.py:38) and later calls it: `if recover and _readonly():` (_mcp_reads.py:173)
advisory.finding: The `/tmp/.ticket-sync-<md5>` throttle markers created during the run are expired at the START of each test but never removed at teardown, leaving stray files in `/tmp` after the suite completes.
merged_from: The MCP fake context passes `readonly=False` as a plain bool, but the real `register_read_tools` treats `ctx.readonly` as a callable gate (`_readonly()` is invoked in the `fsck` tool). This is safe for `list_tickets` (which never calls it) but is a latent trap if the harness is later reused to register/drive a gated tool.

coaching.coaching: Extract the duplicated logic in the throttle-marker cleanup at teardown into a shared helper.
coaching.subject: the throttle-marker cleanup at teardown

coverage.approach_viability_note: 0 surviving high-priority finding(s), Pass-2 drop-rate 50% — the approach (not just nits) may be worth a second look (advisory; the verdict is unchanged).
